pattern: Buying exactly two chocolates as cheaply as possible only ever needs the two smallest prices in the array, since any other pair costs at least as much.
hints: You always want the two cheapest chocolates available -- any other pair can only cost the same or more.
Find the smallest and second-smallest prices in the array, being careful to handle duplicate values correctly.
Compare that minimum possible pair cost against your money: if you can afford it, return the leftover; otherwise you can't buy two chocolates at all, so return money unchanged.

edge_inputs.description: can afford the two cheapest with nothing left over
money exactly covers the cheapest pair
money just one short of the cheapest pair

stress.description: random prices and money across the legal ranges
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 50))
    prices = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    money = rng.randint(1, 100)
    return [prices, money]
